edges.description: Loss of BAP1 disrupts chromatin-associated tumor suppressor programs.
Epigenetic deregulation supports malignant growth of transformed mesothelial cells.
mTOR-pathway activation sustains malignant growth and survival.
Progressive mesothelial proliferation produces diffuse serosal tumor burden.
Tumor burden and associated fluid accumulation increase abdominal girth.
Progressive serosal tumor involvement causes abdominal pain.
Intraperitoneal tumor burden reduces gastric accommodation.
Progressive locoregional spread compromises bowel transit.

nodes.description: Progressive intraperitoneal tumor growth commonly causes abdominal distention.

evidence.reference_title: Malignant Peritoneal Mesothelioma: A Challenging Case for Palliative Care.
evidence.snippet: Malignant peritoneal mesothelioma (MPM) is a rare and very aggressive malignancy of serosal membranes, which typically presents with abdominal pain, distension, and ascites.

nodes.description: Abdominal pain is a common presenting symptom of peritoneal disease.

evidence.reference_title: Diffuse malignant peritoneal mesothelioma--an update on treatment.
evidence.snippet: Presenting symptoms in these patients include: ascites, abdominal pain, asthenia, weight loss, anorexia, abdominal mass, fever, diarrhea and vomiting.

evidence.reference_title: BAP1 Is Altered by Copy Number Loss, Mutation, and/or Loss of Protein Expression in More Than 70% of Malignant Peritoneal Mesotheliomas.
evidence.snippet: Overall, 73% of the malignant peritoneal mesotheliomas analyzed carried at least one inactivated BAP1 allele

nodes.description: BAP1 loss is the dominant recurrent genomic event in peritoneal mesothelioma, often occurring through biallelic inactivation and accompanied by loss of nuclear BAP1 expression.

evidence.reference_title: Genomic profiling of malignant peritoneal mesothelioma reveals recurrent alterations in epigenetic regulatory genes BAP1, SETD2, and DDX3X.
evidence.snippet: The most frequent genetic alteration was biallelic inactivation of the BAP1 gene, which occurred in 9/13 cases, with an additional two cases demonstrating monoallelic loss of BAP1.

evidence.reference_title: BAP1 Is Altered by Copy Number Loss, Mutation, and/or Loss of Protein Expression in More Than 70% of Malignant Peritoneal Mesotheliomas.
evidence.snippet: Overall, 73% of the malignant peritoneal mesotheliomas analyzed carried at least one inactivated BAP1 allele, but only 57% had a complete loss of its protein nuclear expression.

nodes.description: Early satiety reflects locoregional abdominal tumor effects.

evidence.snippet: Morbidity and mortality of the disease are related to progressive locoregional effects within the abdominal cavity, such as distention, pain, early satiety, and decreased oral intake that can ultimately lead to bowel obstruction and cachexia.

nodes.description: Peritoneal mesothelioma recurrently alters epigenetic regulators including BAP1, SETD2, and DDX3X, supporting a transcriptionally deregulated disease program.

evidence.reference_title: Genomic profiling of malignant peritoneal mesothelioma reveals recurrent alterations in epigenetic regulatory genes BAP1, SETD2, and DDX3X.
evidence.snippet: Together, these findings demonstrate the importance of epigenetic modifiers including BAP1, SETD2, and DDX3X in mesothelial tumorigenesis and suggest opportunities for targeted therapies.

nodes.description: Advanced locoregional disease can culminate in bowel obstruction.

evidence.snippet: Morbidity and mortality of the disease are related to progressive locoregional effects within the abdominal cavity, such as distention, pain, early satiety, and decreased oral intake that can ultimately lead to bowel obstruction and cachexia.

nodes.description: Disease morbidity is dominated by progressive locoregional tumor effects within the abdomen rather than early distant metastasis.

evidence.snippet: Morbidity and mortality of the disease are related to progressive locoregional effects within the abdominal cavity, such as distention, pain, early satiety, and decreased oral intake that can ultimately lead to bowel obstruction and cachexia.

nodes.description: Malignant peritoneal mesothelioma expands along the peritoneal surface through progressive proliferation of transformed mesothelial cells.

biological_processes: positive regulation of cell population proliferation

nodes.description: Overactive PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway signaling is a recurrent proliferative program in diffuse malignant peritoneal mesothelioma and provides a mechanistic rationale for targeted therapy development.

evidence.reference_title: Diffuse malignant peritoneal mesothelioma: A review.
evidence.snippet: The overexpression of the phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase (PI3K)/AKT serine/threonine kinase 1 (AKT)/mammalian target of rapamycin (mTOR) signaling pathway drives the malignant phenotype of DMPM
